T0011.title: Scalper Proxy Ordering
T0011.definition: Scalper proxying, where 'scalper' refers to intermediaries who monopolize and resell limited-access goods or participation rights outside legitimate sales channels for profit. The term originated in 20th-century Shanghai, describing ticket scalpers whose coordinated buying sprees were likened to the restlessness of a cattle herd, leading to the label 'scalper' or 'scalper gang'. Such anonymous reselling for profit is illegal in many jurisdictions as it severely disrupts legitimate sales.
T0011.description: Scalper proxying refers to a practice where grey-market operators (large scalpers or goods suppliers controlling distribution channels) hire real individuals to place orders remotely, enabling bulk acquisition of limited-purchase promotional items. The hired individuals (proxy buyers) follow a purchasing script provided by the operator to place orders at designated locations (or later forward them), receiving reimbursement and a commission. The operator then resells the acquired goods for profit.

references.title: [Black Production Big Data] E-commerce scalper industry chain analysis report
